coherence_checks: Same-tag consistency: 'duty', 'prescribed-action', 'operator-system-coupling' — these align with 2.31 and the rest of the karma-yoga block. The doctrinal claim across these tagged verses is consistent.
Cross-reference resolution: Chapter 5 (right-renunciation) and 18.66 (closing release) referenced as correctives for the weaponised-un-replaceability case. Both pointers are doctrinally correct.

Cross-reference support: the citing claim (the verse forbids fleeing duty mid-execution but does not bless every assignment as binding) is supported by Chapter 5's discussion of when withdrawal is appropriate.
Consistency with neighbours: 2.32 said 'the dharmic problem has arrived unsought'; 2.33 says 'if you do not act on it, here are the consequences.' Logical continuity.
verdict_reason: Doctrine coherent across tags and cross-references; consistent with neighbours. PASS on both 6.1 and 6.5.
